steps.title: Locate the main verb already present in the sentence
steps.content: The sentence contains the finite verb “offers” later on, with the same subject (“a compensatory measure”). The structure shows one subject describing two aspects: what the measure does to the organelle's decline, and what explanation it provides.

steps.title: Choose the form that avoids creating two independent clauses
steps.content:
If the blank were a finite verb like “counteracts,” the sentence would create a comma splice—two independent clauses separated only by commas with no coordinating conjunction. “Counteracting” is a participle that modifies “measure” without creating an independent clause, leaving “offers” as the sole main verb.

Using this participle correctly conveys that a compensatory measure counteracting the decline offers a unique explanation of mitochondrial reproduction.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
